_note: This schema uses the human-friendly format (name, options as array, updateSemantics, tags). The API also accepts the native format (propertyName, options as string, update as boolean). Both are auto-normalized on save.
name: Support Ticket Properties
description: Properties extracted from customer support interactions: tickets, chat transcripts, and email threads.

properties.name: Issue Category
properties.description: Primary classification of the support issue. Categorize based on the core problem, not the symptom. If the issue spans multiple categories, choose the most impactful one.

properties.name: Severity
properties.description: Impact severity of the issue. Critical: system down or data loss. High: major feature broken, no workaround. Medium: feature impaired but workaround exists. Low: minor inconvenience or cosmetic issue.

properties.name: Product Area
properties.description: The specific product feature or module affected. Be as specific as possible: 'Dashboard export to CSV' rather than 'Dashboard'. Include the UI location if mentioned.

properties.name: Steps to Reproduce
properties.description: Ordered sequence of actions that trigger the issue. Extract from the customer's description, normalizing into clear steps. Include browser, OS, or environment details if mentioned.

properties.name: Error Message
properties.description: The exact error message or code the customer reported. Preserve the original text verbatim, including error codes, HTTP status codes, or stack trace snippets.

properties.name: Customer Sentiment
properties.description: Overall emotional tone of the customer. Frustrated: expressing anger or dissatisfaction. Concerned: worried but composed. Neutral: factual reporting. Positive: understanding or appreciative despite the issue.

properties.name: Resolution Status
properties.description: Current resolution state. Resolved: fix confirmed by customer. Workaround Provided: temporary solution given. Escalated: sent to engineering or management. Pending: awaiting action. Unresolved: no solution found.

properties.name: Account Tier
properties.description: The customer's subscription or account tier, which affects SLA and priority routing. Extract from the ticket metadata or conversation context.
